annotations.label: Symmetric group (lead)

anchor.snippet: the symmetric group defined over any set is the group whose elements are all the bijections from the set to itself

annotations.note: Equiv.Perm α is the group of bijections (equivalences) of a type to itself, with group structure Equiv.Perm.permGroup.

annotations.label: Order of symmetric group is n!

anchor.snippet: the order (number of elements) of the symmetric group

annotations.note: Fintype.card_perm states card (Perm α) = (card α)!.

annotations.label: Cayley's theorem (lead)

anchor.snippet: Cayley's theorem states that every group

annotations.note: subgroupOfMulAction gives the MulEquiv of any group with a subgroup of the symmetric group on itself.

annotations.label: Symmetric group on a finite set

anchor.snippet: is the group whose elements are all bijective functions from

annotations.note: Equiv.Perm α is precisely the group of bijections from α to itself.

annotations.label: Symmetric group of degree n

anchor.snippet: The symmetric group of degree

annotations.note: S_n is realized as Equiv.Perm (Fin n).

annotations.label: Order is n!

anchor.snippet: elements has order

annotations.note: Fintype.card_perm gives card (Perm α) = (card α)!.

annotations.label: Abelian iff n ≤ 2

anchor.snippet: It is abelian if and only if

annotations.note: No theorem characterizing commutativity of Equiv.Perm by card ≤ 2 was found.

annotations.label: Trivial for empty/singleton set

anchor.snippet: the empty set and the singleton set ), the symmetric groups are trivial

annotations.note: Equiv.permUnique shows Perm α is a unique (trivial) group when α is a subsingleton.

annotations.label: Solvable iff n ≤ 4

anchor.snippet: The group S n is solvable if and only if

annotations.note: Only the n ≥ 5 direction (not solvable) is formalized via Equiv.Perm.not_solvable; solvability for n ≤ 4 is not stated.

annotations.label: Abel–Ruffini consequence

anchor.snippet: This is an essential part of the proof of the

annotations.note: The key non-solvability ingredient exists and a concrete unsolvable quintic is proved in the Archive (Wiedijk100Theorems.AbelRuffini), but not within Mathlib/.

annotations.label: Galois group of general polynomial

anchor.snippet: is the Galois group of the general polynomial of degree n

annotations.note: Mathlib proves an irreducible prime-degree polynomial with two non-real roots has full symmetric Galois group, not the general statement for the generic polynomial.

annotations.label: Coxeter group of type A_n

anchor.snippet: the symmetric group is the Coxeter group of type A n and occurs as the Weyl group

annotations.note: The Coxeter matrix of type Aₙ is defined, but the identification of Equiv.Perm with its Coxeter group is not established in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Elements are permutations

anchor.snippet: are the permutations of X

annotations.note: Elements of Equiv.Perm X are exactly the permutations (self-equivalences) of X.

annotations.label: Group operation is composition

anchor.snippet: The group operation in a symmetric group is function composition

annotations.note: Equiv.Perm.permGroup defines multiplication as composition (trans) of equivalences.

anchors.snippet: The group operation in a symmetric group is function composition

annotations.label: Composition of f and g

anchor.snippet: Applying f after g maps 1 first to 2 and then 2 to itself

annotations.note: Equiv.Perm.mul_apply gives the value of a composite permutation, instantiating such concrete computations.

anchors.snippet: Applying f after g maps 1 first to 2 and then 2 to itself

annotations.label: Cycle taken to k-th power

anchor.snippet: taken to the k th power, will decompose into k cycles of length m

annotations.note: Mathlib characterizes when a power of a cycle is again a cycle (coprimality), but the full gcd-cycle decomposition of a cycle's power is not stated.

anchors.snippet: taken to the k th power, will decompose into k cycles of length m

annotations.label: Closure of composition

anchor.snippet: The operation of function composition is closed in the set of permutations

annotations.note: Closure under composition is part of the group structure Equiv.Perm.permGroup.

annotations.label: Associativity of composition

anchor.snippet: Function composition is always associative.

annotations.note: Associativity is an axiom of the group instance Equiv.Perm.permGroup (mul_assoc).

annotations.label: Identity element

anchor.snippet: serves as an identity for the group

annotations.note: The identity (Equiv.refl, 1) is provided by the group structure Equiv.Perm.permGroup.

annotations.label: Existence of inverses

anchor.snippet: Every bijection has an inverse function that undoes its action

annotations.note: Inverses are given by Equiv.symm and the group inverse in Equiv.Perm.permGroup.

annotations.label: Transposition

anchor.section: Transpositions, sign, and the alternating group
anchor.snippet: A transposition is a permutation which exchanges two elements and keeps all others fixed

annotations.note: Equiv.Perm.IsSwap (via Equiv.swap) is exactly a transposition swapping two elements and fixing the rest.

annotations.label: Every permutation is a product of transpositions

anchor.section: Transpositions, sign, and the alternating group
anchor.snippet: Every permutation can be written as a product of transpositions

annotations.note: closure_isSwap shows the swaps generate the whole permutation group (truncSwapFactors gives an explicit factorization).

annotations.label: Odd and even permutations

anchor.section: Transpositions, sign, and the alternating group
anchor.snippet: it is then called an odd permutation , whereas f is an even permutation

annotations.note: Even/odd are captured by sign f = 1 / sign f = -1 (membership in alternatingGroup = sign.ker).

annotations.label: Invariance of parity

anchor.section: Transpositions, sign, and the alternating group
anchor.snippet: the number of transpositions needed to represent a given permutation is either always even or always odd

annotations.note: sign_prod_list_swap gives sign of a product of swaps = (-1)^length, so parity is well-defined (invariant).

annotations.label: Parity of products

anchor.section: Transpositions, sign, and the alternating group
anchor.snippet: The product of two even permutations is even, the product of two odd permutations is even

annotations.note: sign_mul (sign is multiplicative) encodes the parity rules for products.

annotations.label: Sign of a permutation

anchor.section: Transpositions, sign, and the alternating group
anchor.snippet: Thus we can define the sign of a permutation

annotations.note: Equiv.Perm.sign is the sign homomorphism into ℤˣ.

annotations.label: Sign is a homomorphism

anchor.section: Transpositions, sign, and the alternating group
anchor.snippet: is a group homomorphism

annotations.note: Equiv.Perm.sign is defined as a MonoidHom Perm α →* ℤˣ.

annotations.label: Alternating group A_n

anchor.section: Transpositions, sign, and the alternating group
anchor.snippet: is called the alternating group A n

annotations.note: alternatingGroup α := sign.ker is the alternating group.

annotations.label: A_n normal of index 2

anchor.section: Transpositions, sign, and the alternating group
anchor.snippet: It is a normal subgroup of S n , and for n ≥ 2 it has

annotations.note: alternatingGroup.normal gives normality and alternatingGroup.index_eq_two gives index 2 for nontrivial α.

annotations.label: S_n as semidirect product

anchor.section: Transpositions, sign, and the alternating group
anchor.snippet: The group S n is the semidirect product of A n and any subgroup generated by a single transposition

annotations.note: No semidirect-product decomposition of Equiv.Perm as A_n ⋊ ⟨transposition⟩ is formalized.

annotations.label: Product of adjacent transpositions

anchor.section: Transpositions, sign, and the alternating group
anchor.snippet: every permutation can be written as a product of adjacent transpositions

annotations.note: mclosure_swap_castSucc_succ shows adjacent transpositions generate Perm (Fin n).

annotations.label: Cycle of length k

anchor.snippet: A cycle of length k is a permutation f for which there exists an element x

annotations.note: Equiv.Perm.IsCycle defines a cycle; its length is the cardinality of its support.

annotations.label: Order of a cycle

anchor.snippet: The order of a cycle is equal to its length.

annotations.note: IsCycle.orderOf gives orderOf f = #f.support (its length).

annotations.label: Length-two cycles are transpositions

anchor.snippet: Cycles of length two are transpositions.

annotations.note: card_support_eq_two states #f.support = 2 ↔ IsSwap f.

annotations.label: Disjoint cycles

anchor.snippet: Two cycles are disjoint if they have disjoint subsets of elements.

annotations.note: Equiv.Perm.Disjoint is the disjointness predicate for permutations (disjoint supports).

annotations.label: Disjoint cycles commute

anchor.snippet: Disjoint cycles commute

annotations.note: Disjoint.commute shows any two disjoint permutations (in particular disjoint cycles) commute.

annotations.label: Disjoint cycle decomposition

anchor.snippet: Every element of S n can be written as a product of disjoint cycles

annotations.note: truncCycleFactors (and cycleFactorsFinset) express every permutation as a product of disjoint cycles.

annotations.label: Cycle conjugation property

anchor.snippet: Cycles admit the following conjugation property with any permutation

annotations.note: IsCycle.conj/support_conj and the swap-conjugation lemma Equiv.symm_trans_swap_trans exist, but the explicit elementwise relabeling formula for a general cycle is not a single named lemma.

annotations.label: Order reversing permutation

anchor.snippet: The order reversing permutation is the one given by:

annotations.note: Fin.revPerm is the order-reversing permutation i ↦ n-1-i of Fin n.

annotations.label: Longest element / Bruhat order

anchor.snippet: This is the unique maximal element with respect to the Bruhat order and the longest element

annotations.note: Bruhat order and the longest element of the symmetric group as a Coxeter group are not formalized.

annotations.label: Reversal is an involution

anchor.snippet: This is an involution, and consists of

annotations.note: Fin.rev_rev shows the reversal map is an involution (rev (rev i) = i).

annotations.label: Perfect shuffle

anchor.snippet: the perfect shuffle is the permutation that splits the set into 2 piles and interleaves them

annotations.note: No perfect-shuffle / riffle permutation is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conjugacy classes are cycle types

anchor.snippet: The conjugacy classes of S n correspond to the cycle types of permutations

annotations.note: isConj_iff_cycleType_eq states two permutations are conjugate iff they have the same cycleType.

anchors.snippet: The conjugacy classes of S n correspond to the cycle types of permutations

annotations.label: Conjugacy in S_5

anchor.snippet: For instance, in S 5 , (1 2 3)(4 5) and (1 4 3)(2 5) are conjugate

annotations.note: Such concrete conjugacy facts follow from isConj_iff_cycleType_eq (equal cycle types).

anchors.snippet: For instance, in S 5 , (1 2 3)(4 5) and (1 4 3)(2 5) are conjugate

annotations.label: Classes correspond to partitions

anchor.snippet: Conjugacy classes of S n correspond to integer partitions of n

annotations.note: Equiv.Perm.partition assigns each permutation a partition of card α with partition_eq_of_isConj characterizing conjugacy.

annotations.label: S_0 and S_1 are trivial

anchor.snippet: The symmetric groups on the empty set and the singleton set are trivial

annotations.note: Equiv.permUnique makes Perm α trivial for subsingleton α (Fin 0, Fin 1).

annotations.label: Structure of S_2

anchor.snippet: This group consists of exactly two elements: the identity and the permutation swapping the two points

annotations.note: Fintype.card_perm gives card (Perm (Fin 2)) = 2, the identity and the swap.

annotations.label: S_3 is first nonabelian

anchor.snippet: S 3 is the first nonabelian symmetric group.

annotations.note: No theorem stating S_3 is the smallest nonabelian symmetric group is present.

annotations.label: S_4 and rotations of the cube

anchor.snippet: The group S 4 is isomorphic to the group of proper rotations

annotations.note: No isomorphism between S_4 and the rotation group of the cube is formalized.

annotations.label: Klein four-group in S_4

anchor.snippet: S 4 has a Klein four-group V as a proper normal subgroup

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the Klein four subgroup V and proves it normal/characteristic inside alternatingGroup (Fin 4), not directly as a normal subgroup of S_4.

annotations.label: S_5 is first non-solvable

anchor.snippet: S 5 is the first non-solvable symmetric group.

annotations.note: fin_5_not_solvable proves S_5 is not solvable (the smallest such).

annotations.label: S_6 has outer automorphism

anchor.snippet: Unlike all other symmetric groups, S 6 , has an outer automorphism .

annotations.note: The exceptional outer automorphism of S_6 is not formalized.

annotations.label: Triple covers of A_6 and A_7

anchor.snippet: Note that while A 6 and A 7 have an exceptional Schur multiplier

annotations.note: Exceptional Schur multipliers / triple covers of A_6 and A_7 are not formalized.

annotations.label: Notable homomorphisms between symmetric groups

anchor.snippet: the most notable homomorphisms between symmetric groups, in order of relative dimension

annotations.note: These exceptional homomorphisms between symmetric groups are not formalized.

annotations.label: A_n simple for n ≥ 5

anchor.snippet: the alternating group A n is simple , and the induced quotient is the sign map

annotations.note: alternatingGroup.isSimpleGroup proves A_n is simple when 5 ≤ card α.

annotations.label: S_n semidirect product of A_n and S_2

anchor.snippet: Thus S n is the semidirect product A n

annotations.note: No semidirect product decomposition S_n = A_n ⋊ S_2 is formalized.

annotations.label: Aut(A_n) ≅ S_n for n ≠ 6

anchor.snippet: S n is the full automorphism group of A n

annotations.note: The computation Aut(A_n) ≅ S_n (n ≠ 6) is not formalized.

annotations.label: S_n is a complete group

anchor.snippet: so for n ≠ 2, 6 it is a complete group

annotations.note: Completeness (trivial center and outer automorphism group) of S_n is not formalized.

annotations.label: S_n almost simple for n ≥ 5

anchor.snippet: S n is an almost simple group

annotations.note: Almost-simplicity of S_n is not formalized (no AlmostSimple predicate for S_n).

annotations.label: Embedding of S_n into A_{n+2}

anchor.snippet: S n can be embedded into A n +2 by appending the transposition

annotations.note: The embedding of S_n into A_{n+2} is not formalized.

annotations.label: Generated by adjacent transpositions

anchor.snippet: The symmetric group on n letters is generated by the adjacent transpositions

annotations.note: mclosure_swap_castSucc_succ shows the adjacent transpositions generate Perm (Fin n).

annotations.label: Coxeter group structure

anchor.snippet: This representation endows the symmetric group with the structure of a Coxeter group

annotations.note: Coxeter groups and the type-A matrix exist, and adjacent transpositions generate S_n, but the braid presentation / Coxeter system on Perm is not established.

annotations.label: Other generating sets

anchor.snippet: Other possible generating sets include the set of transpositions that swap 1 and i

annotations.note: It is proved that all transpositions generate S_n, but the specific alternative generating sets (star transpositions, or a transposition plus an n-cycle) are not individually formalized.

annotations.label: Permutation group

anchor.snippet: A subgroup of a symmetric group is called a permutation group .

annotations.note: A permutation group is exactly a Subgroup (Equiv.Perm α), directly expressible though without a dedicated name.

annotations.label: Normal subgroups of finite S_n

anchor.snippet: The alternating group of degree n is always a normal subgroup

annotations.note: A_n is proved normal in S_n, but the full classification of all normal subgroups of finite S_n is not formalized.

annotations.label: No index-2 subgroup on infinite set

anchor.snippet: The symmetric group on an infinite set does not have a subgroup of index 2

annotations.note: No result about index-2 subgroups of the symmetric group on an infinite set is formalized.

annotations.label: Schreier–Ulam theorem

anchor.snippet: The groups A and S are the only nontrivial proper normal subgroups of the symmetric group on a countably infinite set

annotations.note: The Schreier–Ulam classification of normal subgroups of the infinite symmetric group is not formalized.

annotations.label: Three classes of maximal subgroups

anchor.snippet: The maximal subgroups of S n fall into three classes

annotations.note: Only the intransitive case of the O'Nan–Scott classification (stabilizer of a set is maximal) is formalized; the imprimitive and primitive cases are TODO.

annotations.label: Intransitive maximal subgroups

anchor.snippet: The intransitive maximal subgroups are exactly those of the form

annotations.note: isCoatom_stabilizer proves the set-stabilizers (the intransitive maximal subgroups Sym(s)×Sym(sᶜ)) are maximal.

annotations.label: Imprimitive maximal subgroups

anchor.snippet: The imprimitive maximal subgroups are exactly those of the form

annotations.note: The imprimitive case (wreath products as maximal subgroups) is explicitly left as TODO.

annotations.label: Sylow subgroups are p-groups

anchor.snippet: The Sylow subgroups of the symmetric groups are important examples of p -groups

annotations.note: Every Sylow subgroup is a p-group by definition (Sylow.isPGroup'), in particular those of symmetric groups.

annotations.label: Sylow p-subgroups of degree p

anchor.snippet: The Sylow p -subgroups of the symmetric group of degree p are just the cyclic subgroups generated by p -cycles

annotations.note: The n=1 case of mulEquivIteratedWreathProduct gives the Sylow p-subgroup of Perm (card p) as the cyclic group of order p, though the explicit p-cycle generation is not stated separately.

annotations.label: Sylow p-subgroups of degree p^2

anchor.snippet: The Sylow p -subgroups of the symmetric group of degree p 2 are the wreath product of two cyclic groups of order p

annotations.note: The n=2 case of mulEquivIteratedWreathProduct gives the Sylow p-subgroup of Perm (card p²) as the (iterated) wreath product of two cyclic groups of order p.

annotations.label: Iterated wreath product W_p(n)

anchor.snippet: are sometimes denoted W p ( n )

annotations.note: IteratedWreathProduct G n is the n-fold iterated (regular) wreath product.

annotations.label: Sylow p-subgroups of degree n

anchor.snippet: the Sylow p -subgroups of the symmetric group of degree n are a direct product of a i copies of W p ( i )

annotations.note: Mathlib only handles card α = p^n (a single iterated wreath product), not the general degree n as a direct product indexed by the base-p digits.

annotations.label: Transitive subgroup

anchor.snippet: A transitive subgroup of S n is a subgroup whose action on

annotations.note: A transitive subgroup is a subgroup whose induced action satisfies MulAction.IsPretransitive.

annotations.label: Galois group as transitive subgroup

anchor.snippet: the Galois group of a ( finite ) Galois extension is a transitive subgroup of S n

annotations.note: galAction_isPretransitive shows the Galois group of an irreducible polynomial acts transitively on its roots.

annotations.label: Young subgroup

anchor.snippet: A subgroup of S n that is generated by transpositions is called a Young subgroup .

annotations.note: Young subgroups (products of smaller symmetric groups) are not defined in Mathlib (only Young diagrams/tableaux in combinatorics).

annotations.label: Young subgroups are parabolic

anchor.snippet: These groups may also be characterized as the parabolic subgroups of S n

annotations.note: The characterization of Young subgroups as parabolic subgroups is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cayley's theorem

anchor.snippet: Cayley's theorem states that every group G is isomorphic to a subgroup of some symmetric group

annotations.note: subgroupOfMulAction is Cayley's theorem: G ≃* a subgroup of the symmetric group acting on G.

annotations.label: Cyclic subgroup of a symmetric group

anchor.snippet: Cyclic groups are those that are generated by a single permutation.

annotations.note: Subgroup.zpowers g is the cyclic subgroup generated by a single element (e.g. a permutation).

annotations.label: Order is lcm of cycle lengths

anchor.snippet: the order of the cyclic subgroup that it generates is the least common multiple of the lengths of its cycles

annotations.note: lcm_cycleType states σ.cycleType.lcm = orderOf σ.

annotations.label: Largest element order is Landau's function

anchor.snippet: The largest possible order of a cyclic subgroup

annotations.note: Landau's function g(n) (largest element order in S_n) is not formalized.

annotations.label: S_n complete for n ≠ 2, 6

anchor.snippet: For n ≠ 2, 6 , S n is a complete group : its center and outer automorphism group are both trivial.

annotations.note: Completeness of S_n (trivial center and outer automorphisms) is not formalized; only the center of A_n is treated.

annotations.label: Automorphism group for n = 2

anchor.snippet: For n = 2 , the automorphism group is trivial, but S 2 is not trivial

annotations.note: The automorphism group of S_2 is not computed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Outer automorphism for n = 6

anchor.snippet: For n = 6 , it has an outer automorphism of order 2

annotations.note: The order-2 outer automorphism of S_6 is not formalized.

annotations.label: Automorphisms inner except n = 6

anchor.snippet: every automorphism of the symmetric group on X is inner

annotations.note: The theorem that all automorphisms of S_n are inner (except n=6) is not formalized.

annotations.label: First homology of S_n

anchor.snippet: the first homology (concretely, the abelianization ) is:

annotations.note: commutator (Perm α) ≤ alternatingGroup is proved (one inclusion toward abelianization ℤ/2), but the abelianization/first homology of S_n is not explicitly computed.

annotations.label: Second homology (Schur multiplier)

anchor.snippet: The second homology (concretely, the Schur multiplier ) is:

annotations.note: The Schur multiplier (second homology) of S_n is not formalized.

annotations.label: Homological stability

anchor.snippet: in the sense of stable homotopy theory: there is an inclusion map

annotations.note: Homological stability for symmetric groups is not formalized.

annotations.label: Homology of infinite symmetric group

anchor.snippet: The homology of the infinite symmetric group is computed in

annotations.note: The homology of the infinite symmetric group is not formalized.

annotations.label: Number of irreducibles equals partitions

anchor.snippet: the number of inequivalent irreducible representations , over the complex numbers , is equal to the number of partitions of n

annotations.note: The count of irreducible representations of S_n as the number of partitions of n is not formalized.

annotations.label: Irreducibles realized over the integers

anchor.snippet: Each such irreducible representation can be realized over the integers

annotations.note: Rationality/integrality of S_n irreducible representations is not formalized.

annotations.label: Maschke semisimplicity

anchor.snippet: by Maschke's theorem the group algebra K S n is semisimple

annotations.note: Maschke's theorem is formalized as IsSemisimpleModule k[G] (and IsSemisimpleRing k[G]) for any finite group whose order is invertible, including S_n.

annotations.label: Specht modules

anchor.snippet: The modules so constructed are called Specht modules

annotations.note: Specht modules are not defined in Mathlib.
